session_hint: the Kubernetes payment-service OOMKilled pod investigation
question: What approaches did we rule out when investigating the OOMKilled pods in the payment service?
turns: The payment-service pods keep getting OOMKilled with a 512Mi memory limit. We set the JVM heap to -Xmx384m to leave 128Mi headroom. First theory: the application has a memory leak in the transaction processing code.

Ran the service under sustained load for 6 hours and took heap dumps every 30 minutes. Old-gen heap stabilizes at 280Mi with no upward trend. No leak pattern visible in any dump. Application memory leak is ruled out.

Tried reducing JVM heap to -Xmx300m thinking off-heap regions (Metaspace, thread stacks, direct buffers) were consuming the extra headroom. OOMKills continued at the same rate. JVM heap sizing is not the root issue.

Found it: the Envoy proxy sidecar had no memory limit configured. With 500 upstream endpoints registered in xDS, Envoy was consuming 180–220Mi for cluster statistics and endpoint health data. Added a 256Mi limit to the sidecar and bumped the pod memory limit to 768Mi — OOMKills stopped immediately.
